minimal.summary: Creates polished, responsive, standalone HTML slide decks with optional deterministic PNG and image-faithful PPTX export. Use for presentations, pitch decks, reviews, talks, and visual narratives that need browser-native interactivity and portable outputs.
required_inputs: requested output: HTML, PNG, PPTX, or all
target duration or slide count when constrained
evidence and branding requirements when applicable
approved image assets, usage rights, alternative text, and focal crop when applicable
expected_outputs: `Narrative` — the story arc and slide-level claims
`Artifacts` — exact paths to HTML, optional `images/` package, PNG directory, and/or PPTX
`Image Assets` — delivery profile, relative or embedded references, fit behavior, alternative text, and provenance assumptions when images are used
`Interaction` — navigation, notes, fullscreen, and deep-link behavior implemented
`Validation` — slide count, active-state, geometry, console, export-mode, and file checks actually run
`Export Tradeoffs` — platform requirements and image-only PPTX limitation
`Open Assumptions` — illustrative data, missing sources, or content still needing confirmation

notes: wrapper surfaces are additive deployment forms, not capability types
orchestrators must not infer execution authority from wrapper presence alone

install_target.rationale: Source lives inside the current repository, so repo-local installation is the safer default.

minimal.rationale: Source contains reusable workflow/prompt structure with explicit objectives, constraints, or usage framing and is best shipped as a skill surface.
minimal.compatibility: HTML works in modern browsers; bundled PNG exporter requires macOS 13+; PPTX helper requires Python 3.9+.

migration_notes: If the capability lands, compare it against existing entries before apply.
Relationship suggestions are advisory only and do not imply runtime routing.
adjustment_recommendations: add an explicit primary objective before packaging
add deterministic in-scope sections to improve routing fitness

org_graph.work_graph_impact: Adds or updates a skill capability with route profile SSOT_AUDIT.

shared_summary: Creates polished, responsive, standalone HTML slide decks with optional deterministic PNG and image-faithful PPTX export. Use for presentations, pitch decks, reviews, talks, and visual narratives that need browser-native interactivity and portable outputs.
shared_constraints: Keep HTML as the canonical editable source.
Do not invent evidence, quotations, metrics, or sources.
Do not claim export success without validating generated files.
Do not upload a presentation to an external service without an explicit user request.
Do not spawn one browser process per slide when one long-lived renderer can export the deck.
Use only user-provided, locally generated, or explicitly approved image assets; do not silently fetch remote imagery.
Reference packaged images only through relative images/... paths, preserve aspect ratio, and fail export when an image is broken.

modes.mode_summary: Create a polished standalone browser deck with keyboard navigation, hash links, fullscreen, notes, and export mode.
expected_outputs: validated embedded or relative image asset package when images are used

modes.mode_summary: Export one exact-size PNG per validated HTML slide through a single long-lived rendering process.
Assemble verified PNGs into a widescreen image-only PPTX that preserves visual fidelity.
Deliver HTML as the source of truth, then deterministic PNGs, then an image-faithful PPTX.
expected_outputs: validated embedded or relative image asset package when images are used

preferred_use_cases: visually faithful PNG and PPTX delivery

historical_baseline.reason: No git history was available, so the current SSOT body is the strongest available baseline.
